title: AI-SDLC OrchestratorEvent
description:
One JSONL line written by the autonomous-pipeline orchestrator's events writer (RFC-0015 Phase 4 / AISDLC-169.4). Lines accrete to date-rotated files at $ARTIFACTS_DIR/_orchestrator/events-YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl. The stream is the canonical bus for downstream consumers (cli-status --orchestrator, future web dashboard, Slack push, etc.) per RFC §7.3 — 'the file exists, it's append-only, it's schema-stable.' Phase 4 ships the writer + the loop wiring + the cli-status view + this schema; Phase 5 (AISDLC-169.5) adds the corpus + chaos-test surface.

Discriminator: the `type` field. Common envelope fields (`ts`, optional `taskId`, optional `runId`) ride on every event; per-type properties live in their own oneOf branch. The schema is intentionally extensible — new event types added by future RFC-0015 phases (or downstream RFCs) extend the `OrchestratorEventType` enum without requiring a v2 bump, since unknown `type` values are rejected by validators that enforce the enum strictly + permitted by validators that don't.

ts.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the event was minted at. Always set by the writer at append time so consumers don't need to derive it from the rotated file's date suffix.

taskId.description: Canonical task ID this event refers to, e.g. 'AISDLC-169.4'. Optional — orchestrator-level events (`OrchestratorTick`) have no task scope.

runId.description: Orchestrator session UUID — stable across all ticks within one `runOrchestratorLoop()` invocation. Lets consumers correlate the events emitted by a single orchestrator process even when the date-rotated file rolls over mid-run.

tick.description: Tick number this event was emitted in (0-indexed). Present on tick + dispatch + completion/failure events, omitted on out-of-band events (e.g. `WorkerStateTransition` emitted by a long-poll handler outside the tick loop).

workerId.description: Worker identifier (e.g. `w-aisdlc-169.4`). Present on worker-scoped events (`WorkerStateTransition`).

from.description: Previous WorkerState — present on `WorkerStateTransition` only. See `pipeline-cli/src/orchestrator/playbook/types.ts#WorkerState` for the canonical state list.

duration_ms.description: Wall-clock duration the worker spent in `from` before transitioning to `to`. Present on `WorkerStateTransition`.

outcome.description: Pipeline outcome enum value (`approved`, `needs-human-attention`, `unknown-failure`, etc.) — present on `OrchestratorCompleted`.

prUrl.description: PR URL associated with the event when one exists — present on `OrchestratorCompleted` / `OrchestratorFailed` / `OrchestratorRecovered`.

reason.description: Free-form human-readable reason — present on `OrchestratorFailed` (escalation reason) / `OrchestratorAwaitingExternal` (filter trace).

dispatched.description: Tasks actually dispatched in this tick — present on `OrchestratorTick`.

context.description: Free-form structured context bag for the event. Schema-less by design so future event types can carry per-type payload without a v2 bump.

blockers.description: Upstream task IDs blocking the candidate — present on `OrchestratorBlockedByDependency` (RFC-0015 Phase 3 / AISDLC-169.3).

signedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the most recent DoR calibration signature for the task — present on `OrchestratorBlockedByDor`. Null when no signature exists.

allExternalDeps.description: All declared external deps for this candidate (cleared + uncleared) — present on `OrchestratorAwaitingExternal`.

idleStreak.description: Consecutive idle ticks since the last dispatch — present on `OrchestratorIdleNoWork` / `OrchestratorIdleAllFiltered`.

rejectedCount.description: Number of candidates rejected by the filter chain this tick — present on `OrchestratorIdleAllFiltered`.

ticksSinceFirstSkip.description: Consecutive-skip count for the candidate at the moment the stuck event fires — present on `OrchestratorStuckCandidate`.

completedChildren.description: IDs of the candidate's children that are all already in `backlog/completed/` (lowercased, sorted). At least one entry by construction. Present on `OrchestratorOrphanParent` (AISDLC-175) — emitted when the orphan-parent filter rejects a parent task whose every declared child has already shipped, so the orchestrator stops dispatching developer subagents for what is bookkeeping (parent file `git mv` to `completed/`) the framework should handle.

startedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the existing in-flight dispatch was started — present on `OrchestratorTaskAlreadyInFlight` (RFC-0015 / AISDLC-179). Lets operators correlate the rejected re-dispatch attempt with the original dispatch's `OrchestratorDispatched` event.

initialOutputPreview.description: Truncated (≤1000 chars) raw output the developer subagent returned on the FIRST turn that violated the JSON envelope contract — present on `DeveloperContractRetry` (AISDLC-176). Lets operators see the prose the dev produced before the re-emission retry recovered the dispatch.

retryDurationMs.description: Wall-clock duration in ms the retry spawn took (from re-emission prompt issue to parsed JSON envelope) — present on `DeveloperContractRetry` (AISDLC-176).

phase.description: Discriminator identifying which dispatch path emitted the retry-recovery event — present on `DeveloperContractRetry` (AISDLC-196). `'initial'` = Step 5b/6 first-call dispatch (iteration 1); `'iteration'` = Step 9 iteration loop, iterations N>1 (after a CHANGES_REQUESTED round). Without this discriminator operators grepping events.jsonl cannot attribute retries to the initial-dispatch path versus the iteration-loop path; the recovery-frequency story is meaningfully different on each (initial-path drift = developer.md system prompt regression; iteration-path drift = post-feedback re-dispatch fragility).

iteration.description: Iteration loop counter when the retry recovered — present on `DeveloperContractRetry` events with `phase === 'iteration'` (AISDLC-196). Always >=2 by construction: iteration 1 is the initial dispatch (which emits `phase: 'initial'` instead). Lets operators bucket iteration-path retries by which round of feedback re-dispatch produced the prose.

fromStatus.description: Task status before Step 4 flipped it (typically `To Do`). Present on `OrchestratorRollback` (AISDLC-177).

toStatus.description: Status the orchestrator restored the task to during rollback (matches `fromStatus`). Present on `OrchestratorRollback` (AISDLC-177). NOTE: `toStatus` mirrors `fromStatus` even when the file write failed — consult `statusReverted` (AISDLC-186) to know whether the on-disk file actually changed.

statusReverted.description: True when the task file's `status:` line was successfully patched back to `fromStatus`; false when the rollback could not write the file (e.g. task file disappeared mid-run, frontmatter became unparseable). Present on `OrchestratorRollback` (AISDLC-186). When false, the operator must manually reset the task status — `toStatus` reports the INTENDED post-rollback status, not the OBSERVED on-disk value.

worktreeRemoved.description: True when `git worktree remove --force <path>` succeeded during rollback. Present on `OrchestratorRollback` (AISDLC-177).

branchQuarantined.description: True when the failed dispatch's branch carried commits beyond `origin/main` AND the orchestrator preserved them under a `quarantine/<ref>` ref. Present on `OrchestratorRollback` (AISDLC-177).

quarantineRef.description: Quarantine ref name (e.g. `quarantine/aisdlc-70-2026-05-04T14-23-44-123`) the orchestrator created when preserving the failed dispatch's commits. Present on `OrchestratorRollback` (when `branchQuarantined` is true) and on `OrchestratorWorkQuarantined` (AISDLC-177). The timestamp suffix carries millisecond precision (AISDLC-186); legacy second-precision refs from pre-186 rollbacks (`...T14-23-44`) co-exist with the new format.

branch.description: Original branch name the dev was working on before the rollback. Present on `OrchestratorWorkQuarantined` (AISDLC-177).

commitSha.description: SHA of the tip commit preserved under the quarantine ref. Present on `OrchestratorWorkQuarantined` (AISDLC-177).

commitCount.description: Number of commits beyond `origin/main` on the quarantined branch. Always at least 1 by construction (a zero-commit branch isn't quarantined). Present on `OrchestratorWorkQuarantined` (AISDLC-177).

until.description: Advisory ISO date after which the operator should re-evaluate the block. Present on `TaskBlocked` (AISDLC-223) when the task's `blocked.until` frontmatter field is set.

unblockedBy.description: Optional list of task IDs that, when all reach Done, advise the operator to clear the block. Present on `TaskBlocked` (AISDLC-223) when the task's `blocked.unblockedBy` frontmatter field is set. Phase 2 (AC #8 of AISDLC-223) will emit an additional `TaskUnblockExpired` advisory event when all listed task IDs are Done; until then this field is informational only.

hadOpenPR.description: Safety-predicate outcome: whether an open GitHub PR was found for the branch at the time of the auto-cleanup decision. Always `false` when cleanup proceeded (an open PR would have blocked cleanup). Present on `WorktreeAutoCleaned` (AISDLC-224).

hadUncommittedChanges.description: Safety-predicate outcome: whether the existing worktree directory had uncommitted changes at the time of the auto-cleanup decision. Always `false` when cleanup proceeded (uncommitted changes would have blocked cleanup). Present on `WorktreeAutoCleaned` (AISDLC-224).

worktreePath.description: Absolute path of the worktree directory that was removed. Present on `OrchestratorWorktreeSwept` (AISDLC-256).

mergedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp (or 'unknown') when the PR was merged. Present on `OrchestratorWorktreeSwept` (AISDLC-256).

bucket.description: Stage A candidate bucket — RFC-0016 §4.1 t-shirt size. Present on `EstimateCaptured` (AISDLC-280).

finalBucket.description: Final bucket after Stage A + (Phase 4+) Stage B reconciliation. In Phase 2 always equals `bucket`. Present on `EstimateCaptured` (AISDLC-280).

estimateInputHash.description: RFC-0016 §8.4 content hash over `(taskTitle, taskDescription, stageA_signals, classAssignment)`. Present on `EstimateCaptured` and as `newHash` on `EstimateInputChanged` (AISDLC-280).

runIndex.description: Ensemble run index (1, 2, 3 …) for repeated runs against the same `estimateInputHash`. Present on `EstimateCaptured` (AISDLC-280).

confidence.description: RFC-0016 §5.2 Stage A confidence rating. Present on `EstimateCaptured` (AISDLC-280).

escalateToStageB.description: RFC-0016 §5.2 Phase 4 hook — true when Stage A signals split across non-adjacent buckets. Present on `EstimateCaptured` (AISDLC-280).

oldHash.description: Previous `estimateInputHash` for the task. Present on `EstimateInputChanged` (AISDLC-280).

newHash.description: New `estimateInputHash` for the task — same value as the next `EstimateCaptured` event's hash. Present on `EstimateInputChanged` (AISDLC-280).

prOpenedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the PR was opened. Present on `PrOpened` (AISDLC-493).

rebased.description: Whether the reconcile pass performed a git rebase. Present on `ReconcileCompleted` (AISDLC-493).

reSignCount.description: Number of attestation re-sign operations performed during this reconcile pass. Present on `ReconcileCompleted` (AISDLC-493).

reconcileDurationMs.description: Wall-clock duration of the reconcile pass in milliseconds. Present on `ReconcileCompleted` (AISDLC-493).

dispatchedAt.description: ISO-8601 dispatch timestamp for the task. Present on `DispatchToMergeCompleted` (AISDLC-493).

totalLifecycleMs.description: Total dispatch→merge wall-clock duration in milliseconds. Present on `DispatchToMergeCompleted` (AISDLC-493).

ciWaitMs.description: Best-effort CI-wait duration in milliseconds derived retroactively from gh run list at merge-discovery time. Null when CI timing data is unavailable. Present on `DispatchToMergeCompleted` (AISDLC-493).

OrchestratorEventType.description: Discriminator. Phase 4 (AISDLC-169.4) shipped the seven core types covering tick lifecycle + dispatch outcomes + worker-state transitions + the external-deps filter rejection. Phase 3 (AISDLC-169.3) extends the enum with the remaining five filter-rejection / idle / stuck event types so the events.jsonl stream is the single observability path. AISDLC-175 adds `OrchestratorOrphanParent` for parent-task closure detection. AISDLC-176 adds `DeveloperContractRetry` for the recovery path when the developer subagent returns non-JSON prose and the retry-once helper recovers the dispatch. AISDLC-196 extends `DeveloperContractRetry` with `phase` (`'initial' | 'iteration'`) + optional `iteration` (present when `phase === 'iteration'`) so operators can attribute recovery events to the initial-dispatch path versus the iteration-loop path — additive non-breaking change. AISDLC-223 adds `TaskBlocked` emitted on every tick that the Blocked admission filter rejects a candidate (the task has a non-empty `blocked.reason` frontmatter field). AISDLC-224 adds `WorktreeAutoCleaned` for the Step 3 auto-cleanup path (stale branch self-heal in autonomous mode). AISDLC-493 adds `PrOpened` (PR lifecycle anchor), `ReconcileCompleted` (per-pass reconcile overhead), and `DispatchToMergeCompleted` (DORA lead-time join). Future phases / RFCs extend this enum without a schema bump (consumers that don't enforce the enum strictly will tolerate unknown types, those that do will reject + log).
